title: Uragoga coriacea
taxonomy: Uragoga coriacea is classified within the family Annonaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This classification places Uragoga coriacea alongside other members of the Annonaceae family, including custard apple and soursop. The genus Uragoga is a part of this family, and Uragoga coriacea is one of its species. The exact classification of Uragoga coriacea is further detailed in its binomial nomenclature, which consists of the genus and species names. Uragoga coriacea is also referred to by its full classification as Uragoga coriacea (Engl.) Mildbr., which includes its author and the year it was described.
uses: Uragoga coriacea is a plant species native to tropical Africa, and its bark has been traditionally used for medicinal purposes. The plant's bark contains compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions. In traditional medicine, Uragoga coriacea is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's bark is also used to create a poultice, which is applied topically to relieve pain and reduce swelling. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.
